description: Fix BIND_TO_DELETE hook format and ensure /smmway command handler is robust. The BIND_TO_DELETE is currently assigned as a bare function (on_delete) instead of a list [on_delete] like the other BIND_TO hooks. This inconsistency can cause FPC to fail when iterating hooks. Also verify the /smmway command handler is properly structured.
steps: In /projects/sandbox/smmway/smmway_v1.3.11 (2).py, at line 3753, change 'BIND_TO_DELETE = on_delete' to 'BIND_TO_DELETE = [on_delete]' to match the pattern used by BIND_TO_PRE_INIT, BIND_TO_NEW_ORDER, and BIND_TO_NEW_MESSAGE (all use lists)

Verify the /smmway command handler in init_tg_menu() (around line 2635) is correctly defined with @bot.message_handler(commands=['smmway', 'smm', 'smmway_menu']) -- this already exists but confirm it works by checking the function structure
Verify the COMMANDS dict (line 65) includes 'smmway', 'smm', 'smmway_menu' entries -- already present
Run py_compile to verify no syntax errors after the fix
acceptance_criteria: BIND_TO_DELETE is a list containing on_delete function, consistent with other BIND_TO hooks
File compiles without syntax errors (py_compile succeeds)
The /smmway command handler exists in init_tg_menu() and responds to commands smmway, smm, smmway_menu
COMMANDS dict has entries for smmway, smm, smmway_menu
verification: python3 -c "import ast; tree = ast.parse(open('smmway_v1.3.11 (2).py').read()); print('AST OK')"
